actions.name: post_companies

custom_fields.description: Key value pairs containing the names and values of custom fields. Only dates in the format YYYY-MM-DD are accepted as input for custom date fields. Read more here

domains.description: Domains of the company. Email addresses of contacts that contain this domain will be associated with that company automatically.

note.description: Any specific note about the company

health_score.description: The strength of your relationship with the company

account_tier.description: Classification based on how much value the company brings to your business

renewal_date.description: Date when your contract or relationship with the company is due for renewal

industry.description: The industry the company serves in

lookup_parameter.description: This attribute for companies can only be set if Custom Objects feature is enabled. The value can either be in the form of the display_id (record id) or primary_field_value (user defined record value). The default value is display_id.

actions.risk: Freshdesk Create a Company (POST /companies); requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute.

actions.name: post_contacts

email.description: Primary email address of the contact. If you want to associate additional email(s) with this contact, use the other_emails attribute.

social_handler.description: Social handles of Contact. Up to 10 values can be defined. Valid Facebook, Instagram and Twitter (X) handles supported.

other_emails.description: Additional emails associated with the contact

company_id.description: ID of the primary company to which this contact belongs

view_all_tickets.description: Set to true if the contact can see all the tickets that are associated with the company to which he belong

other_companies.description: Additional companies associated with the contact. This attribute can only be set if the Multiple Companies feature is enabled (Estate plan and above)

avatar.description: Avatar image of the contact The maximum file size is 5MB and the supported file types are .jpg, .jpeg, .jpe, and .png

custom_fields.description: Key value pairs containing the name and value of the custom field. Only dates in the format YYYY-MM-DD are accepted as input for custom date fields. Read more here

description.description: A small description of the contact

language.description: Language of the contact. Default language is "en" . This attribute can only be set if the Multiple Language feature is enabled (Garden plan and above)

time_zone.description: Time zone of the contact. Default value is the time zone of the domain . This attribute can only be set if the Multiple Time Zone feature is enabled (Garden plan and above)

lookup_parameter.description: This attribute for contacts can only be set if the Custom Objects feature is enabled. The value can either be in the form of the display_id (record id) or primary_field_value (user defined record value). The default value is display_id.

actions.risk: Freshdesk Create a Contact (POST /contacts); requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute.

actions.name: post_contacts_export

fields.description: Fields of the contacts to be exported

actions.risk: Freshdesk Contact Export (POST /contacts/export); requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute.

actions.name: post_contacts_merge

contact.description: Contains attributes that need to be updated in the primary contact during merge (optional)

actions.risk: Freshdesk Merge Contacts (POST /contacts/merge); requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute.

actions.name: post_tickets

requester_id.description: User ID of the requester. For existing contacts, the requester_id can be passed instead of the requester's email.

email.description: Email address of the requester. If no contact exists with this email address in Freshdesk, it will be added as a new contact.

facebook_id.description: Facebook ID of the requester. A contact should exist with this facebook_id in Freshdesk.

phone.description: Phone number of the requester. If no contact exists with this phone number in Freshdesk, it will be added as a new contact. If the phone number is set and the email address is not, then the name attribute is mandatory.

twitter_id.description: Twitter handle of the requester. If no contact exists with this handle in Freshdesk, it will be added as a new contact.

unique_external_id.description: External ID of the requester. If no contact exists with this external ID in Freshdesk, they will be added as a new contact.

subject.description: Subject of the ticket. The default Value is null.

type.description: Helps categorize the ticket according to the different kinds of issues your support team deals with. The default Value is null.

status.description: Status of the ticket. The default Value is 2.

priority.description: Priority of the ticket. The default value is 1.

responder_id.description: ID of the agent to whom the ticket has been assigned

attachments.description: Ticket attachments. The total size of these attachments cannot exceed 20MB.

cc_emails.description: Email address added in the 'cc' field of the incoming ticket email

custom_fields.description: Key value pairs containing the names and values of custom fields. Read more here

due_by.description: The response will be "null" unless values are provided in the payload. The due_by value is set within a few seconds of ticket creation.

email_config_id.description: ID of email config which is used for this ticket. (i.e., support@yourcompany.com/sales@yourcompany.com) If product_id is given and email_config_id is not given, product's primary email_config_id will be set

fr_due_by.description: The response will be "null" unless values are provided in the payload. The fr_due_by value is set within a few seconds of ticket creation.

group_id.description: ID of the group to which the ticket has been assigned. The default value is the ID of the group that is associated with the given email_config_id

parent_id.description: ID of the parent ticket that this ticket should be linked to. When passing this field, the current ticket actioned upon will be converted to a child ticket.

product_id.description: ID of the product to which the ticket is associated. It will be ignored if the email_config_id attribute is set in the request.

source.description: The channel through which the ticket was created. The default value is 2.

tags.description: Tags that have been associated with the ticket

company_id.description: Company ID of the requester. This attribute can only be set if the Multiple Companies feature is enabled (Estate plan and above)

internal_agent_id.description: ID of the internal agent which the ticket should be assigned with

internal_group_id.description: ID of the internal group to which the ticket should be assigned with

lookup_parameter.description: This attribute for tickets can only be set if Custom Objects is enabled and a lookup field has been added under ticket fields.The value can either be in the form of the display_id (record id) or primary_field_value (user defined record valu

actions.risk: Freshdesk Create a Ticket (POST /tickets); requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute.

actions.name: post_tickets_bulk_delete
actions.risk: Freshdesk Delete Multiple Tickets (POST /tickets/bulk_delete); requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute.

actions.name: post_tickets_bulk_update
actions.risk: Freshdesk Update Multiple Tickets (POST /tickets/bulk_update); requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute.

actions.name: post_tickets_outbound_email

email.description: Email address of the requester. If no contact exists with this email address in Freshdesk, it will be added as a new contact.

subject.description: Subject of the ticket. The default Value is null.

type.description: Helps categorize the ticket according to the different kinds of issues your support team deals with. The default Value is null.

status.description: Status of the ticket. The default Value is 5.

priority.description: Priority of the ticket. The default value is 1.

attachments.description: Ticket attachments. The total size of these attachments cannot exceed 20MB.

custom_fields.description: Key value pairs containing the names and values of custom fields. Read more here

due_by.description: The response will be "null" unless values are provided in the payload. The due_by value is set within a few seconds of ticket creation.

email_config_id.description: ID of email config which is used for this ticket. (i.e., support@yourcompany.com/sales@yourcompany.com)

fr_due_by.description: The response will be "null" unless values are provided in the payload. The fr_due_by value is set within a few seconds of ticket creation.

group_id.description: ID of the group to which the ticket has been assigned. The default value is the ID of the group that is associated with the given email_config_id

tags.description: Tags that have been associated with the ticket

actions.risk: Freshdesk Create an Outbound Email (POST /tickets/outbound_email); requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute.

actions.name: post_tickets_id_forward
actions.risk: Freshdesk Forward a ticket (POST /tickets/{id}/forward); requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute.

actions.name: put_agents_id

occasional.description: Set to true if this is an occasional agent (true => occasional, false => full-time)

signature.description: Signature of the agent in HTML format

ticket_scope.description: Ticket permission of the agent (1 -> Global Access, 2 -> Group Access, 3 -> Restricted Access). Current logged in agent can't update his/her ticket_scope

skill_ids.description: Skill ids associated with the agent

group_ids.description: Group IDs associated with the agent

contribution_group_ids.description: IDs of groups that the agent has view-only access to, if the ticket_scope of the agent is 2

role_ids.description: Role IDs associated with the agent. At least one role should be associated with the agent. Current logged in agent can't update his/her role_ids (Role IDs should be corresponding to the type of agents. For example: Ticket collaborator for c

language.description: Language of the Agent. Default language is "en"

time_zone.description: Time zone of the Agent. Default value is time zone of the domain

focus_mode.description: Focus mode of the agent. Default value is true

actions.risk: Freshdesk Update an Agent (PUT /agents/{id}); requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute.

occasional.description: Set to true if this is an occasional agent (true => occasional, false => full-time)

signature.description: Signature of the agent in HTML format

ticket_scope.description: Ticket permission of the agent (1 -> Global Access, 2 -> Group Access, 3 -> Restricted Access). Current logged in agent can't update his/her ticket_scope

role_ids.description: Role IDs associated with the agent. At least one role should be associated with the agent. Current logged in agent can't update his/her role_ids (Role IDs should be corresponding to the type of agents. For example: Ticket collaborator for c

type.description: Type of Agent (support_agent -> Support Agent, field_agent -> Field Agent, collaborator -> Collaborator) By default, agent will be converted to a Support Agent

focus_mode.description: Focus mode of the agent. Default value is true

actions.name: put_groups_id

agent_ids.description: Array of agent user ids separated by comma (' , '). Instructions on getting the agent's user ID

auto_ticket_assign.description: Describes the automatic ticket assignment type. Will not be supported if your Freshdesk account is not on a plan that supports automatic ticket assignment. The accepted values are 0 and 1. To set other assignment types to a group, use Admin

escalate_to.description: The user to whom the escalation email is sent of a ticket is unassigned. To create/update escalate_to with 'none' provide the value 'null' in the request

unassigned_for.description: The time after which an escalation email will be sent if a ticket remains unassigned. The accepted values are "30m" for 30 minutes, "1h" for 1 hour, "2h" for 2 hour, "4h" for 4 hour, "8h" for 8 hour, "12h" for 12 hour, "1d" for 1 day, "2d"

actions.name: put_time_entries_id

agent_id.description: The agent to whom this time-entry is associated. One agent can have only one timer running. Everything else will be stopped if new timer is on for an agent

billable.description: Set as true if the entry is billable. Default value is true

executed_at.description: Time at which this time-entry id added/created

start_time.description: The time at which the time-entry is added or the time of the last invoked "start-timer" action using a toggle

time_spent.description: The number of hours (in hh:mm format). Used to set the total time_spent

timer_running.description: Indicates if the timer is running

actions.risk: Freshdesk Update a Time Entry (PUT /time_entries/{id}); requires reverse ETL plan, preview, approval, and execute.
